description: Negative/Distractor Resilience Benchmark — 22 seeds with homonym pairs (Python lang/snake, Java lang/island/coffee, Spring framework/season/mechanical, Rust lang/corrosion, etc.). 15 queries with explicit grade-0 distractors that should NOT be retrieved. Tests false-positive avoidance.

distractorAnalysis.description: Custom metric: how often grade-0 distractors appear in top-5 results
observations: d-q01 (Python ML): d-python-snake appears at rank 2 in vector mode — homonym not fully separated
d-q02 (Python snake): d-python-lang appears at rank 2 — symmetric leakage, expected for homonyms
d-q03 (Java+Spring enterprise): d-java-island leaks in at rank 4 in vector, d-spring-mechanical at rank 4-5
d-q05 (Mars rover): d-mars-candy appears at rank 2 across all modes — strong false positive
d-q09 (iron corrosion): d-rust-lang appears at rank 2 — Rust programming concept leaks
d-q13 (LaTeX typesetting): d-latex-material appears at rank 3 — moderate leakage
d-q15 (toxic metals): d-rust-lang appears in results — 'rust' triggers programming association

weakQueries.query: Dependency injection and inversion of control in application frameworks
weakQueries.analysis: Only finds d-spring-framework. Query is too technical/specific — only 1 relevant seed out of 3 possible grades.

weakQueries.query: Hooke's law and elastic deformation in mechanical systems
weakQueries.analysis: Only finds d-spring-mechanical. Single relevant seed, recall denominator includes only 1 relevant entry.

weakQueries.query: Memory safety and ownership in systems programming languages
weakQueries.analysis: Finds d-rust-lang but d-rust-corrosion not retrieved as distractor (good). Low recall because only 1 relevant seed.

keyFindings: nDCG is extremely high (0.988) — when the correct answer is found, it's always ranked #1 (perfect MRR for vector modes)
Recall is lower (0.723-0.737) because many queries have only 1 relevant seed — the denominator includes grade-0 entries
Distractors frequently appear in top-5 but NEVER at rank 1 — the system distinguishes the correct sense for top ranking
BM25 hybrid mode slightly degrades MRR (0.933) — keyword overlap on homonyms introduces noise
Mars candy bar is the strongest false positive — appears at rank 2 in all modes for the planet query
Programming language homonyms (Python, Rust, Java) consistently leak into top-5 results for non-tech queries
